intelligence.hypothesis: Insufficient telemetry; awaiting next valid capture.

compositional_truth_check.findings: P3 (Pothos) is present with anchor. P1 (String of Nickels) is present but shows no visible foliage. P2/P4 (Mexican Mint/Silver Guest) are not identifiable as distinct specimens; the black pot contains only soil and debris.

plant_audit.P1: Yellow pot contains soil and a plant marker, but no visible String of Nickels foliage.
plant_audit.P2_P4: Black pot contains soil and eggshell fragments; no mint or silver guest foliage detected.
plant_audit.P3: Pothos specimen remains stable, anchored by the white rabbit figurine.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Consistently dark and moist across all pots.
biome_observations.debris: Eggshell fragments present in the black pot (P2/P4). Electronic component (IC chip) observed on the desk surface, shifting position across the timeline.

temporal_deltas.T-5_to_T-1: The electronic component on the desk surface moved from the right side to the bottom right, then disappeared/was removed by T-1.
temporal_deltas.Current: The desk surface is clear of the electronic component; plant states remain static with no new growth or further decay observed.

visual_health_inference.P1: Dormant or failed; requires investigation into root viability.
visual_health_inference.P2_P4: Critical/Dead; substrate appears devoid of biological activity.
visual_health_inference.P3: Stable; the Pothos maintains its posture and leaf integrity.

anomalies: Presence of electronic component (IC chip) on desk surface.
Eggshell fragments used as soil amendment/top dressing.
Systemic loss of P2 and P4 specimens.
vision_report.narrative_description: The biome is currently in a state of arrested development. While the Pothos (P3) remains a stable anchor, the other specimens (P1, P2, P4) show no signs of active growth and are effectively non-functional in the current registry. The presence of eggshells suggests an attempt at nutrient supplementation, but the lack of foliage in the black pots indicates a failure to thrive or complete specimen loss. The desk environment is stable, though the movement of the electronic component suggests external human intervention.
